question: I have never heard of Intelligent Robotics. What is this degree about?
expected_answer: The Bachelor of Science (Honours) Intelligent Robotics is a 3-year programme that strikes an exquisite balance between the fundamentals of engineering and hands-on practical skills. This multidisciplinary programme combines electronics, robotics, artificial intelligence, automation, and computer programming. It adopts a modern learning approach with early exposure to real-world applications. Graduates will be agile knowledge workers in the IR4.0 age and beyond, highly sought after by the industry.

question: Show the full subject outline for ARM6113 Technical Calculus.?
expected_answer: Subject Code: ARM6113 Title: Technical Calculus Category: core Group: technical Year: 1 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2026 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 48 hours (Lecture Hours = 35 and Supervised Tutorial Hours = 13) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide necessary grounding in calculus, algebra, vectors, complex functions, Fourier analysis and probability to students pursuing engineering and science degrees. Assessment: - Tests/Quizzes: 40% - Final Examination: 60% Course Contents: - Calculus of one variable - Fourier series - Complex number functions and vector algebra - Probability Course Learning Outcomes: - Compute the limits, derivatives, integrals and extrema of single-variable functions. - Perform basic vector operations and calculate the powers and roots of complex numbers. - Construct the power series and the Fourier series representations of functions. - Determine the probability, mean and variance for various distributions in discrete and continuous random variables. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx

question: Show the full subject outline for ARL6113 Electrical Circuits.?
expected_answer: Subject Code: ARL6113 Title: Electrical Circuits Category: core Group: technical Year: 1 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2026 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 46 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide a basic understanding of the laws of circuit theory and the behaviour of circuits during steady-state and transient conditions. Assessment: - Test/Quiz: 20% - Assignment: 20% - Lab: 10% - Final Examination: 50% Laboratory: - CK1: Thevenin’s Theorem and Superposition Theorem - CK2: AC Circuits Course Contents: - Circuit elements and signal waveforms - Basic laws of electric circuit - Network analysis - Sinusoidal steady state analysis - Transient analysis Course Learning Outcomes: - Explain the basic concepts and laws of electric circuits, and different types of signals. - Solve the electrical networks using nodal and mesh analysis techniques. - Compute the impedance, resonance and complex power of sinusoidal circuits. - Analyse the transient response of RL, RC and RLC circuits. - Construct a circuit by using circuit theory experiment board to verify the law of electric circuit and phasor relationship. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx

question: Show the full subject outline for ARE6113 Basic Electronics.?
expected_answer: Subject Code: ARE6113 Title: Basic Electronics Category: core Group: technical Year: 1 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2026 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 47 hours (lectures, tutorials & laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide a sound understanding of basic electronic devices such as diodes, transistors, and their respective applications. Assessment: - Lab: 10% - Assignment: 15% - Test/Quiz: 15% - Final Examination: 60% Laboratory: - Diode Circuits - Transistor Circuits Course Contents: - Fundamentals of semiconductor devices - P-N junction characteristics and diode models - Diode applications - Bipolar junction transistor and biasing techniques - Transistor amplifier and small-signal equivalent circuits Course Learning Outcomes: - Explain the fundamental properties of semiconductor material and characteristics of PN junction as circuit element. - Analyse the diode and bipolar junction transistor (BJT) circuits. - Analyse the transistor amplifier circuits. - Demonstrate the diode and transistor circuits. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx

question: Show the full subject outline for ARM6123 Differential Equations.?
expected_answer: Subject Code: ARM6123 Title: Differential Equations Category: core Group: technical Year: 1 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2026 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 47 hours (Lecture Hours = 34 and Supervised Tutorial Hours = 13) Pre-Requisite: ARM6113 Technical Calculus Objective: To introduce students to differential equations with focus on the ordinary differential equations and partial differential equations, as well as the requisite concepts and analysis tools to understand and solve them, including partial differentiation and integral transforms. Assessment: - Tests/Quizzes: 40% - Final Examination: 60% Course Contents: - Ordinary differential equations - Partial differentiation - Integral transform - Partial differential equations Course Learning Outcomes: - Solve differential equations using various methods. - Apply concepts of calculus to solve multivariable problems. - Determine appropriate integral transforms to solve boundary or initial value problem, and difference equations. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Prerequisite taken from programme structure.

question: Show the full subject outline for ARE6123 Digital Design.?
expected_answer: Subject Code: ARE6123 Title: Digital Design Category: core Group: technical Year: 1 Trimester: 3 Intake Cycle Label: October/November 2026 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 47 hours (lectures, tutorials & laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide a basic understanding of the basics of logic circuits and their applications in digital system. Assessment: - Lab: 10% - Assignment: 15% - Test/Quiz: 15% - Final Examination: 60% Laboratory: - DL1: Logic Gates And Their Applications - DL2: Flip-flop And Their Applications Course Contents: - Introduction - Number systems and codes - Algebraic methods for analysis and synthesis of logic circuits - Simplification of switching functions - Introduction to sequential logic - Modular combinational logic Course Learning Outcomes: - Distinguish the analog and digital systems, apply positional notations, number systems and computer codes in digital systems. - Use Boolean algebra, the Karnaugh maps and Quine-McCluskey tabular methods to simplify and design logic circuits. - Apply the concepts of sequential logic and memory devices in digital systems. - Design modular combinational circuits using encoders, decoders, multiplexers and demultiplexers. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx

question: Show the full subject outline for ARC6123 Micro-controllers & Micro-processors.?
expected_answer: Subject Code: ARC6123 Title: Micro-controllers & Micro-processors Aliases: Micro-controllers and micro-processors, Microcontrollers and Microprocessors Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 45 hours (lectures, tutorials & laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provide a practical introduction to microcontrollers and microprocessors, assembly language programming techniques, interface hardware design, and microcontroller and microprocessor system design considerations. Assessment: - Test: 20% - Assignment: 20% - Lab Test: 20% - Final exam: 40% Course Contents: - Introduction - Kinematics fundamentals - Linkage synthesis - Engineering materials - Screws, fasteners and nonpermanent joints - Bearings, couplings and lubrication - Flexible mechanical elements Course Learning Outcomes: - Describe the fundamental of linkage mechanisms. - Choose suitable engineering materials based on criteria such as strength, flexibility, machinability, cost and weight. - Evaluate suitable mechanical systems which contain the combination of motors, gear trains, linkages and other moving parts. - Build a mechanical system which contain the combination of motors, gear trains, linkages and other moving parts. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx

question: Show the full subject outline for ARL6153 Feedback Control.?
expected_answer: Subject Code: ARL6153 Title: Feedback Control Aliases: Feedback control Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 45 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To introduce time domain, root locus and frequency domain methods for analysis and design of feedback control systems. Assessment: - Test: 15% - Assignment: 15% - Lab: 10% - Final exam: 60% Course Contents: - Introduction to control systems - System modelling - Time domain analysis of control systems - Root locus method - Frequency domain analysis - Compensation techniques Course Learning Outcomes: - Apply differential equations and transfer functions to the modelling of physical systems. - Analyse transient response, error and stability of control systems using time domain and root locus methods. - Analyse properties of control systems using frequency domain methods. - Design compensators for control systems using strategic thinking. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx

question: Show the full subject outline for ARL6123 Electrical Machines and Power Systems.?
expected_answer: Subject Code: ARL6123 Title: Electrical Machines and Power Systems Aliases: Electrical Machines and Power System Category: core Group: technical Year: 2 Trimester: 3 Intake Cycle Label: October/November 2027 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 47 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To introduce the basic concepts and working principles of electrical machines and transformers as well as electrical power systems and their protection methods. Assessment: - Test: 10% - Assignment: 20% - Lab Assessment: 20% - Final exam: 50% Course Contents: - Introduction to machinery principles - Three-phase circuits - Transformers - D.C. machines - AC machines (Induction Motors & Synchronous Motors) Course Learning Outcomes: - Apply the machinery principles and balanced three-phase power system circuit analysis on electrical machines and transformers. - Analyze the circuit characteristics and performance of transformers. - Analyze the circuit characteristics and performance of DC and AC machines. - Demonstrate the characteristics and performance of single-phase transformer and induction motor. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Normalized title to plural 'Systems'.

question: Show the full subject outline for ARP6110-P1 Project I.?
expected_answer: Subject Code: ARP6110-P1 Title: Project I Aliases: ARP6110, Project Category: core Group: technical Year: 3 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2028 Credit Hours: 5 Contact Hours: 35 hours Pre-Requisite: Completed 60 credit hours (excluding Art & Humanities subjects) Objective: To enable students to apply knowledge and skills gained from different courses through solving of real world problems; acquire technical skills through literature review, analysis, design, implementation, and testing/evaluation; and develop soft skills needed to carry out a real world project. Assessment: - Part 1 – General Effort, Project Design, Research Proposal: 25% - Part 1 – Presentation: 10% Course Contents: - Project planning, analysis and design - Research proposal - Presentation Course Learning Outcomes: - Develop the strategies for background study, analysis, design, implementation, and evaluation based on real-world problem. - Demonstrate communication skills in writing and presenting the project design and implementation. - Employ contemporary digital and numeracy tools to critically analyze problems in artificial intelligence related fields. - Demonstrate self-awareness in continuous lifelong-learning skills. - Apply integrated problem solving to propose a research paper/proposal for commercialization of the product. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Normalized from single ARP6110 code in structure.

question: Show the full subject outline for ARR6143 Robotics – Modelling and Control.?
expected_answer: Subject Code: ARR6143 Title: Robotics – Modelling and Control Aliases: Robotics - Modelling and Control Category: core Group: technical Year: 3 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2028 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 44 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provides students with rigorous mathematical skills on modelling and control of robotic arm and similar manipulator. Assessment: - Test: 20% - Assignment: 20% - Lab Test: 20% - Final exam: 40% Laboratory: Control and automation lab, Robotic arms. Course Contents: - Representing position and orientation - Robot modeling - Forward and inverse kinematics - Trajectory generation - Velocity relationships - Dynamic and control Course Learning Outcomes: - Compute the pose of a robotic arm given the configuration, links and angle of each joint. - Deduce the joint angle of each robotic arm link given the required pose via inverse kinematic. - Generate the trajectory for the end-effector of a robotic arm via joint-space or Cartesian space schemes using anticipatory thinking. - Construct linear trajectory-following control scheme for robotic arm. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx

Assessment: - Test: 20% - Assignment: 20% - Lab/Demonstration: 20% - Project: 40% Course Contents: - Introduction to mobile robots and drones - Wheeled robots - Balancing and legged robots - Drones Course Learning Outcomes: - Discuss the essential components and building blocks of mobile robots and drones. - Construct mobile robots or drones for solving a practical problem. - Develop mobile robots or drones using anticipatory thinking to perform a specific task. - Demonstrate entrepreneurial skills by writing a proposal for commercialization of the product/solution. - Demonstrate leadership, autonomy and responsibility in solving a practical problem. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Prerequisite overridden by programme structure.

question: Show the full subject outline for ARC6184 Making Embedded Systems.?
expected_answer: Subject Code: ARC6184 Title: Making Embedded Systems Category: core Group: technical Year: 3 Trimester: 3 Intake Cycle Label: October/November 2028 Credit Hours: 4 Contact Hours: 60 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: ARC6123 Micro-controllers & Micro-processors Objective: To introduce the best practice and the art of putting together an embedded system, from selection of processors, basic hardware requirements, to software design. Assessment: - Quiz: 20% - Test: 20% - Lab Test: 30% - Project: 30% Course Contents: - Introduction - Creating a system architecture - Getting on the hardware - Inputs, outputs and timers - Managing the flow of activity - Communicating with peripherals - Doing more with less - PCB design and layout - PCB fabrication, soldering and reworking Course Learning Outcomes: - Describe the hardware and software requirements of a robust embedded systems from datasheet. - Construct an embedded controller with support circuit from scratch. - Develop a software framework using systematic thinking for the embedded controller to perform a useful task. - Design PCB layout for an electronic prototype. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Prerequisite overridden by programme structure.

question: Show the full subject outline for ART6116 Industrial Training.?
expected_answer: Subject Code: ART6116 Title: Industrial Training Category: industrial_training Group: technical Year: 3 Trimester: 3 Intake Cycle Label: October/November 2028 Credit Hours: 6 Contact Hours: 90 hours Pre-Requisite: Completed 60 credit hours Objective: To expose students to the real working environment and get acquainted with organization structure, business operations and administrative functions; to gain hands-on experience in related fields; and to foster collaboration between industry and university. Assessment: - Performance assessed by company supervisor: 20% - Logbook assessed by company supervisor: 20% - Lecturer’s visit: 20% - Oral Presentation: 20% - Report: 20% Course Contents: - Industrial training - Passing all five assessment components separately to accumulate six credit hours Course Learning Outcomes: - Express technical experience and solutions in written or oral forms. - Demonstrate the codes of ethics and the responsibilities of engineering practice. - Use digital and numeracy skills or other relevant tools to solve problems. - Demonstrate the industrial best practices and entrepreneur principles in a team. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx

question: Show the full subject outline for LMPU3312 Integrity and Leadership.?
expected_answer: Subject Code: LMPU3312 Title: Integrity and Leadership Category: mpu Group: university Year: 1 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2026 Credit Hours: 2 Contact Hours: 28 hours Pre-Requisite: N.A. Objective: The course aims to spread the importance of integrity and anti-corruption practices among the young generation before they enter the workforce. Assessment: - Quiz: 40% - Project: 60% Course Contents: - Corruption and anti-corruption - Agencies related to anti-corruption and integrity - SPRM Act 2009 - White collar crime - Factor, symptom and impact of corruption - Social institution roles in integrity culture - Reporting corruption, preventive and government initiatives - Auditor General's Report and common issues in government procurement - Corporate liability and leadership liability - Leadership with professional integrity Course Learning Outcomes: - Apply the concept of integrity and anti-corruption in daily activities. - Demonstrate ability to lead and work collaboratively. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: LMPU3312 — Integrity and Leadership.pdf

question: Show the full subject outline for LMPU3192 Falsafah dan Isu Semasa.?
expected_answer: Subject Code: LMPU3192 Title: Falsafah dan Isu Semasa Aliases: Philosophy and Current Issues Category: mpu Group: university Year: 1 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2026 Credit Hours: 2 Contact Hours: 26 hours Pre-Requisite: Nil Objective: To equip students to think critically and logically, to debate analytically and methodically, and to respond more sensibly and judiciously to the different challenges of current issues and ideologies. Assessment: - Assignment 1: 30% - Presentation: 20% - Quiz: 20% - Assignment 2: 30% Course Contents: - Introduction to philosophy - Metaphysics - Epistemology - Ideology - Philosophy in life - Logic - Psychology and sociology - Decolonization - Selected case study on philosophy and current issues Course Learning Outcomes: - Explain current issues based on philosophy, National Philosophy of Education and Rukun Negara. - Explain current issues based on the main flow of thinking in various philosophies. - Analyze current issues through a comparative perspective of philosophy as a basis for intercultural dialogue. Assessment: - Assignment 1: 30% - Presentation: 20% - Quiz: 20% - Assignment 2: 30% Course Contents: - Pengenalan Penghayatan Etika dan Peradaban Dalam Acuan Malaysia - Konsep Etika dan Peradaban - Etika dan Peradaban Dalam Masyarakat Kepelbagaian - Warisan dan Pendigitalan Sejagat - Pemantapan Kesepaduan Nasional Malaysia - Pembinaan Peradaban Majmuk di Malaysia - Teknologi Maklumat dan Komunikasi Penggerak Kesepaduan Nasional di Malaysia - Peranan Etika dan Peradaban Mendokong Tanggungjawab Sosial di Malaysia - Cabaran Kelestarian Etika dan Peradaban di Malaysia - Perlembagaan sebagai Tapak Integrasi dan Wahana Etika dan Peradaban di Malaysia Course Learning Outcomes: - Menjelaskan konsep etika dan peradaban dalam konteks penghayatannya mengikut acuan Malaysia. - Menganalisis sistem, tahap perkembangan, kemajuan sosial dan kebudayaan merentas etnik. - Membincangkan isu kontemporari berkaitan ekonomi, politik, sosial, budaya dan alam sekitar daripada perspektif etika dan peradaban. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: LMPU3182 — Penghayatan Etika dan Peradaban.pdf Notes: - Local-student MPU-U1 option.

question: Show the full subject outline for LSSW6112 Sustainable Society.?
expected_answer: Subject Code: LSSW6112 Title: Sustainable Society Category: university_course Group: university Year: 3 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2028 Credit Hours: 2 Contact Hours: 28 hours Pre-Requisite: None Objective: To equip students with a comprehensive understanding of Environmental, Social, and Corporate Governance (ESG) principles within the broader context. Assessment: - Quiz: 20% - Project: 80% Course Contents: - Definition and significance of ESG - Global ESG trends and standards - Environmental aspects - Climate change and business - Sustainable resource management - Social factors - Corporate social responsibility - Diversity and inclusion - Corporate governance - Ethical decision-making Course Learning Outcomes: - Apply the basic knowledge of ESG. - Demonstrate caring, ethical, mindful, and sustainable attributes through active participation in the ESG project. question: Show the full subject outline for LDCW6123 Fundamentals of Digital Competence for Programmers.?
expected_answer: Subject Code: LDCW6123 Title: Fundamentals of Digital Competence for Programmers Category: university_course Group: university Year: 3 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2028 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 41 hours Pre-Requisite: None Objective: The course aims to introduce the knowledge and skills of the digital competence and its framework elements. It also exposes learners to the emergence of digital competence as a new approach and skills needed to manage daily life tasks and industry. Assessment: - Assignment 1: 30% - Assignment 2: 30% - Project: 40% Course Contents: - Digital competence and its framework - Information and data literacy - Communication and collaboration - Digital content creation - Safety in digital world - Problem solving in digital world - Distributed version control and Git workflows Course Learning Outcomes: - Describe measures for digital safety and solutions for common digital problems in a modern workplace environment. - Demonstrate ability to work with digital information collaboratively through collaboration and communication tools. - Build a program collaboratively with digital content. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: LDCW6123 — Fundamentals of Digital Competence for Programmers.pdf

expected_answer: The Bachelor of Science (Honours) Intelligent Robotics is a 3-year programme that strikes an exquisite balance between the fundamentals of engineering and hands-on practical skills. question: What are the course learning outcomes for ARL6113 Electrical Circuits?
expected_answer: Course learning outcomes for ARL6113 Electrical Circuits: Explain the basic concepts and laws of electric circuits, and different types of signals.; Solve the electrical networks using nodal and mesh analysis techniques.; Compute the impedance, resonance and complex power of sinusoidal circuits.; Analyse the transient response of RL, RC and RLC circuits.; Construct a circuit by using circuit theory experiment board to verify the law of electric circuit and phasor relationship.

question: What should a student know from ARL6113 Electrical Circuits?
expected_answer: ARL6113 Electrical Circuits objective: To provide a basic understanding of the laws of circuit theory and the behaviour of circuits during steady-state and transient conditions. Key contents: Circuit elements and signal waveforms; Basic laws of electric circuit; Network analysis; Sinusoidal steady state analysis; Transient analysis Learning outcomes: Explain the basic concepts and laws of electric circuits, and different types of signals.; Solve the electrical networks using nodal and mesh analysis techniques.; Compute the impedance, resonance and complex power of sinusoidal circuits.; Analyse the transient response of RL, RC and RLC circuits.; Construct a circuit by using circuit theory experiment board to verify the law of electric circuit and phasor relationship.

expected_answer: The Bachelor of Science (Honours) Intelligent Robotics is a 3-year programme that strikes an exquisite balance between the fundamentals of engineering and hands-on practical skills.
